### Query Breakdown

| # | Resource | Concept | Code | Rationale |
|---|----------|---------|------|-----------|
| 1 | `Condition` | Hyperthyroidism | SNOMED `34486009` | General hyperthyroidism diagnosis |

| 2 | `MedicationRequest` | Methimazole | RxNorm `8153` | Primary antithyroid drug; prescription implies active disease management |
| 3 | `Observation` | TSH | LOINC `3016-3` | Thyrotropin \[Units/vol\] in Serum or Plasma |

### Key Design Decisions

- **Query 1 — Comma-separated codes:** FHIR interprets comma-separated values within a single `code=` parameter as a logical **OR**, so one query captures both SNOMED concepts without a second round-trip.
- **Query 2 — Ingredient-level RxNorm:** RxNorm `8153` is the ingredient concept for methimazole, matching all formulations and strengths regardless of how the drug was prescribed.
- **Query 3 — Suppressed TSH threshold:** `lt0.1 m[IU]/L` targets biochemically suppressed TSH (< 0.1 mIU/L), the accepted clinical cutoff distinguishing overt hyperthyroidism and capturing subclinical cases that lack a formal diagnosis code.
- **Union logic:** Patients surfaced by **any** of the three queries are included; deduplication on `Patient.id` is applied downstream after collecting all hits.

### Query Breakdown

| Parameter | Value | Rationale |
|-----------|-------|-----------|
| `Resource` | `Condition` | Clinical diagnosis records |
| `code` | `http://snomed.info/sct\|34486009` | **Hyperthyroidism (disorder)** — the broad general concept covering all causes of excess thyroid hormone |
| `code` | `http://snomed.info/sct\|353295004` | **Graves' disease (disorder)** — the autoimmune (TSH-receptor antibody–mediated) subtype; clinicians often document this specific etiology rather than the parent term |

### Design Notes

- **Single query with comma-separated codes** — FHIR interprets comma-separated values on the same parameter as a logical **OR**, so either code will match, avoiding the need for two round trips.
- **Graves' disease included intentionally** — it is physiologically a form of hyperthyroidism but may be coded at this more specific level; excluding it would silently miss a large portion of autoimmune hyperthyroid patients.
- **Hypothyroidism explicitly excluded** — SNOMED `40930008` (Hypothyroidism) and related codes are the *opposite* condition and are **not** included.
- To also capture active-only diagnoses, append `&clinical-status=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-clinical|active`.
- To link back to patients, append `&_include=Condition:subject`.

## FHIR Query: Suppressed TSH (Hyperthyroidism Screen)

**Key Parameters:**
- **LOINC `3016-3`** — *Thyrotropin [Units/volume] in Serum or Plasma* (the canonical TSH code)
- **Threshold `< 0.4 m[IU]/L`** — captures both subclinical (0.1–0.4) and overt (< 0.1) suppression
- **`status=final`** — excludes preliminary/amended/entered-in-error results
- **`_include=Observation:subject`** — pulls Patient resources alongside matching observations

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|3016-3&value-quantity=lt0.4||m[IU]/L&status=final&_include=Observation:subject
```

### Clinical & Technical Notes

| Aspect | Detail |
|---|---|
| **LOINC code** | `3016-3` (Thyrotropin [Units/volume] in Serum or Plasma) |
| **Suppression threshold** | `< 0.4 m[IU]/L` (below the conventional lower reference limit) |
| **UCUM unit** | `m[IU]/L` *(equivalent to `[uIU]/mL`; numerically identical — adjust if your system stores the latter)* |
| **Subclinical hyperthyroid** | TSH 0.1–0.39 m[IU]/L with normal FT4/FT3 |
| **Overt hyperthyroid** | TSH < 0.1 m[IU]/L (markedly undetectable) |
| **False positives to consider** | Euthyroid sick syndrome, exogenous thyroid hormone, dopamine/glucocorticoids, first-trimester pregnancy |

> **Unit variation:** If your lab stores TSH in `[uIU]/mL`, the numeric values are identical — substitute `lt0.4||[uIU]/mL` in the `value-quantity` parameter. To be safe across both representations, run both queries in parallel.
